title: Intersection of Two Linked Lists
problem_statement: Find the node where two singly linked lists intersect, or return null if they do not.

examples.explanation: The lists intersect at the node with value 8.

constraints: The number of nodes is in the range [0, 10^5]
intuition: Use two pointers that traverse both lists; when one pointer finishes, switch it to the other list.

approaches.name: Two-pointer switching
approaches.description: Traverse both lists with two pointers. After reaching the end, switch to the other list so they align at the intersection.
approaches.why_not: A hash set solution works but uses extra memory.

interview_qa.q: Why switch pointers when they reach the end?
interview_qa.a: Switching equalizes path lengths so both pointers traverse the same total distance.

interview_qa.q: What if there is no intersection?
interview_qa.a: Both pointers will become null and the loop ends.
